findings.evidence: grep of DifferentialSymmetry.lean shows only GridChain.transposeEquiv/_apply/_single and GridChain.rotateEquiv/_apply/_single; no relabelEquiv reference anywhere outside Complex.lean.
findings.fix: Mark relabelEquiv private. transposeEquiv/rotateEquiv are not @[expose], so their bodies (and the proof terms of their lemmas) can reference a private relabelEquiv fine. Drop it from the Main definitions docstring.
findings.issue: relabelEquiv is a new public definition (and listed in Main definitions) but no named downstream target uses it; it is only the factored implementation helper behind transposeEquiv/rotateEquiv. This over-exposes surface to hypothetical users.

findings.evidence: relabelEquiv never appears in any consumer term, so its simp lemmas cannot fire outside Complex.lean; the wrapper lemmas at lines 98-108, 128-138 already carry @[simp].
findings.fix: Make these lemmas private (and drop @[simp], or use local scoping); the transpose/rotate wrappers remain the public simp normal-form lemmas.
findings.issue: relabelEquiv_single, relabelEquiv_apply, relabelEquiv_symm are @[simp] but only serve to prove the already-@[simp] transposeEquiv/rotateEquiv lemmas; with relabelEquiv non-public and never appearing in consumer goals, these global simp lemmas are redundant public API.

summary: Sound refactor, but the new shared primitive GridChain.relabelEquiv (and its three @[simp] lemmas) is public surface with no named downstream consumer — DifferentialSymmetry.lean uses only transposeEquiv/rotateEquiv. It is an implementation helper for the two involution equivs and should be private.
